edges.description: Abnormal Cav1.4 channel function impairs calcium-dependent signaling at rod and cone photoreceptor synapses.
Photoreceptor-to-bipolar synaptic dysfunction produces reduced photopic and scotopic retinal responses on electroretinography.
edges.target: Retinal Signal Transmission and ERG Abnormality

edges.description: Abnormal retinal signal transmission manifests clinically as poor acuity, nystagmus, myopia, color-vision defects, and impaired dark adaptation.
edges.source: Retinal Signal Transmission and ERG Abnormality

edges.description: The retinal signal-transmission defect is measured clinically as the characteristic abnormal electroretinogram.

edges.description: The refractive phenotype includes progressive axial myopia.
The retinal channelopathy phenotype includes color-vision defects.
The retinal visual-function phenotype includes nystagmus.
The retinal visual-function phenotype includes reduced acuity.
The retinal structural phenotype includes foveal hypoplasia.
The retinal structural phenotype includes fundus hypopigmentation.
The retinal signal defect manifests as impaired dark adaptation.
edges.target: Difficulty adjusting from light to dark
